commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier AI lab, security cloud, or developer platform gets the bridge from agentic AI control evidence to operational SOC detection without needing customers to expose raw prompts.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Hosted secure-context event pipeline, signed detection pack delivery, SIEM/SOAR integrations, alert-to-receipt correlation, and managed replay validation.
commercialization_path.open_layer: Public SOC detection model, SIEM query templates, runtime evaluator, docs, and generated evidence pack.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Private tenant field mappings, collector configuration, alert routing, suppression lists, ticket enrichment, and response runbooks.

detection_rules.response_playbook: Kill the session, quarantine the context source, run replay fixtures, and require source recertification before retrieval resumes.

siem_queries.microsoft_sentinel_kql:
AgenticAIEvents
| where EventClass in ("secure_context.retrieval", "context.poisoning_scan", "policy.decision")
| where (tostring(Attributes["poisoning_scan_state"]) in ("critical", "high", "failed_open") or tostring(Attributes["context.retrieval_decision"]) in ("deny_context_poisoning", "kill_session_on_context_poisoning"))
| extend DetectionRule="context-poisoning-retrieval", Severity="critical"
siem_queries.splunk_spl: index=agentic_ai (event_class="secure_context.retrieval" OR event_class="context.poisoning_scan" OR event_class="policy.decision") ((poisoning_scan_state="critical" OR poisoning_scan_state="high" OR poisoning_scan_state="failed_open") OR (context.retrieval_decision="deny_context_poisoning" OR context.retrieval_decision="kill_session_on_context_poisoning"))

detection_rules.title: Context poisoning reached retrieval

mapped_risks: OWASP MCP07 insufficient authentication and authorization
detection_rules.response_playbook: Kill the agent session, revoke delegated credentials, preserve the run receipt, and open an MCP authorization incident.

siem_queries.microsoft_sentinel_kql:
AgenticAIEvents
| where EventClass in ("mcp.tools.call", "policy.decision")
| where (tolower(tostring(Attributes["authorization.token_passthrough_detected"])) in ("true", "1", "yes") or tolower(tostring(Attributes["authorization.audience_mismatch"])) in ("true", "1", "yes") or tostring(Attributes["authorization.decision"]) in ("deny_token_passthrough", "kill_token_passthrough", "deny_audience_mismatch"))
| extend DetectionRule="mcp-token-passthrough-or-audience-mismatch", Severity="critical"
siem_queries.splunk_spl: index=agentic_ai (event_class="mcp.tools.call" OR event_class="policy.decision") (authorization.token_passthrough_detected=true OR authorization.audience_mismatch=true OR (authorization.decision="deny_token_passthrough" OR authorization.decision="kill_token_passthrough" OR authorization.decision="deny_audience_mismatch"))

detection_rules.title: MCP token passthrough or audience mismatch

detection_rules.response_playbook: Kill the session, purge unsafe telemetry, rotate exposed credentials, and preserve metadata-only incident evidence.

siem_queries.microsoft_sentinel_kql:
AgenticAIEvents
| where (tolower(tostring(Attributes["telemetry.contains_secret"])) in ("true", "1", "yes") or tolower(tostring(Attributes["telemetry.cross_tenant_context"])) in ("true", "1", "yes") or tostring(Attributes["telemetry.redaction_state"]) in ("raw_secret", "unredacted_pii", "cross_tenant"))
| extend DetectionRule="secret-or-cross-tenant-telemetry", Severity="critical"
siem_queries.splunk_spl: index=agentic_ai (telemetry.contains_secret=true OR telemetry.cross_tenant_context=true OR (telemetry.redaction_state="raw_secret" OR telemetry.redaction_state="unredacted_pii" OR telemetry.redaction_state="cross_tenant"))

detection_rules.title: Secret or cross-tenant data emitted into telemetry

detection_rules.response_playbook: Hold or kill the action, require explicit reviewer approval, and attach the failed approval receipt to incident evidence.

siem_queries.microsoft_sentinel_kql:
AgenticAIEvents
| where tostring(Attributes["action.impact_class"]) in ("high", "critical", "irreversible")
| where tostring(Attributes["approval.decision"]) in ("missing", "expired", "declined", "bypassed")
| extend DetectionRule="approval-bypass-high-impact-action", Severity="high"
siem_queries.splunk_spl: index=agentic_ai (action.impact_class="high" OR action.impact_class="critical" OR action.impact_class="irreversible") (approval.decision="missing" OR approval.decision="expired" OR approval.decision="declined" OR approval.decision="bypassed")

detection_rules.title: High-impact action without valid approval receipt

detection_rules.response_playbook: Stop browser automation, preserve the URL/form evidence, redact sensitive values, and replay the boundary case.

siem_queries.microsoft_sentinel_kql:
AgenticAIEvents
| where tostring(Attributes["destination_class"]) in ("external", "public_internet", "unknown")
| where (tostring(Attributes["egress.decision"]) in ("deny_context_egress", "kill_session_on_egress", "hold_for_egress_review") or tolower(tostring(Attributes["browser.prompt_injection_signal"])) in ("true", "1", "yes") or tolower(tostring(Attributes["browser.sends_external_message"])) in ("true", "1", "yes"))
| extend DetectionRule="browser-agent-url-exfiltration", Severity="high"
siem_queries.splunk_spl: index=agentic_ai (destination_class="external" OR destination_class="public_internet" OR destination_class="unknown") ((egress.decision="deny_context_egress" OR egress.decision="kill_session_on_egress" OR egress.decision="hold_for_egress_review") OR browser.prompt_injection_signal=true OR browser.sends_external_message=true)

detection_rules.title: Browser agent URL or form-based data exfiltration

detection_rules.response_playbook: Freeze the connector promotion path, compare signed descriptors, and require human review before tool calls resume.

siem_queries.microsoft_sentinel_kql:
AgenticAIEvents
| where tolower(tostring(Attributes["tool_surface.drift_detected"])) in ("true", "1", "yes")
| where (tostring(Attributes["tool_surface.drift_severity"]) in ("critical", "high") or tostring(Attributes["policy.decision"]) in ("hold_for_tool_surface_review", "deny_tool_surface_drift", "kill_session_on_tool_surface_drift"))
| extend DetectionRule="mcp-tool-surface-drift-critical", Severity="high"
siem_queries.splunk_spl: index=agentic_ai tool_surface.drift_detected=true ((tool_surface.drift_severity="critical" OR tool_surface.drift_severity="high") OR (policy.decision="hold_for_tool_surface_review" OR policy.decision="deny_tool_surface_drift" OR policy.decision="kill_session_on_tool_surface_drift"))

detection_rules.title: Critical MCP tool surface drift

detection_rules.response_playbook: Block release or connector promotion, attach replay evidence, and route to the owning control pack maintainer.

siem_queries.microsoft_sentinel_kql:
AgenticAIEvents
| where tostring(Attributes["replay.status"]) in ("failed", "regressed")
| where (tostring(Attributes["replay.observed_decision"]) != tostring(Attributes["replay.expected_decision"]) or tostring(Attributes["policy.decision"]) in ("hold_for_replay_failure", "deny_release_on_replay_regression"))
| extend DetectionRule="red-team-replay-regression", Severity="high"

detection_rules.title: Red-team replay regression after control change

detection_rules.response_playbook: Quarantine the server, run connector intake, compare advertised tools, and require registry promotion before reuse.

siem_queries.microsoft_sentinel_kql:
AgenticAIEvents
| where tostring(Attributes["mcp.server.registry_status"]) in ("unknown", "unregistered", "quarantined")
| where (tostring(Attributes["mcp.server.trust_tier"]) in ("unknown", "experimental", "blocked") or tostring(Attributes["policy.decision"]) in ("deny_unregistered_connector", "hold_for_connector_intake", "kill_session_on_shadow_mcp"))
| extend DetectionRule="shadow-mcp-server-or-unknown-connector", Severity="high"
siem_queries.splunk_spl: index=agentic_ai (mcp.server.registry_status="unknown" OR mcp.server.registry_status="unregistered" OR mcp.server.registry_status="quarantined") ((mcp.server.trust_tier="unknown" OR mcp.server.trust_tier="experimental" OR mcp.server.trust_tier="blocked") OR (policy.decision="deny_unregistered_connector" OR policy.decision="hold_for_connector_intake" OR policy.decision="kill_session_on_shadow_mcp"))

detection_rules.title: Shadow MCP server or unknown connector used by agent

detection_rules.response_playbook: Refresh source packs, rerun generators, and flag control mappings whose source anchor has changed.

siem_queries.microsoft_sentinel_kql:
AgenticAIEvents
| where (tostring(Attributes["source.freshness_decision"]) in ("stale", "hold_for_source_refresh", "standards_changed") or tolower(tostring(Attributes["standards.changed"])) in ("true", "1", "yes"))
| extend DetectionRule="source-freshness-or-standard-drift", Severity="medium"

detection_rules.title: Source freshness or agentic standard drift

detection_rules.response_playbook: Pause the run, summarize loop state, require reviewer continuation, and tune workflow budget or termination criteria.

siem_queries.microsoft_sentinel_kql:
AgenticAIEvents
| where (todouble(tostring(Attributes["loop.iteration_count"])) >= 8 or todouble(tostring(Attributes["budget.tokens_pct"])) >= 90 or todouble(tostring(Attributes["budget.cost_pct"])) >= 90)
| extend DetectionRule="unbounded-agent-loop-or-cost-runaway", Severity="medium"

detection_rules.title: Unbounded agent loop or cost runaway

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes can convert secure-context controls into deployable detections for agentic AI and MCP incidents while preserving least-data telemetry defaults.
default_questions_answered: Which agentic and MCP events should the SOC alert on first?
Which telemetry fields are required before an alert is actionable?
Which detections force kill-session, escalation, investigation, or no-alert decisions?
How do alerts tie back to run receipts, policy decisions, context packages, incidents, and replay evidence?
Which SIEM query shape should Splunk, Sentinel, and Chronicle users start with?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach the pack to agentic AI SIEM onboarding, MCP gateway logging requirements, SOC playbooks, design-partner pilots, and acquisition diligence.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with open detection rules, then sell hosted event normalization, SIEM/SOAR export, receipt correlation, tenant-specific suppression, and managed replay validation.

intent: Define a vendor-neutral SOC detection profile for agentic AI and MCP systems so SecurityRecipes can turn secure-context telemetry, policy decisions, incident classes, red-team replay, and threat-radar signals into SIEM-ready detections without requiring raw prompt or tool payload collection.

positioning.buyer: Detection Engineering, AI Platform, Security Operations, Security Engineering, GRC, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.category: Agentic AI SOC detection and secure-context signal pack
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes makes agentic AI observable in the systems enterprises already run: each detection maps to trace fields, MCP policy evidence, run receipts, incident response, and SOC-ready queries.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI has moved from chat to tool-using systems with identities, MCP servers, browser sessions, external egress, and non-human approvals. Security teams need detections they can deploy before autonomous agents become an unmanaged production blind spot.

residual_risks.risk: The open pack provides portable detection logic, not a guarantee that a customer's collector emits every required attribute.
residual_risks.treatment: Use the Agentic Telemetry Contract and this SOC pack together during SIEM onboarding.

residual_risks.risk: Query templates require field mapping before they are production-ready in any specific SIEM.
residual_risks.treatment: Bind SecurityRecipes field names to tenant collector schemas and keep raw prompt/tool payload capture disabled by default.

residual_risks.risk: Agentic attack chains evolve quickly as MCP servers, browser agents, and model capabilities change.
residual_risks.treatment: Regenerate the pack after source-freshness or threat-radar changes and replay red-team fixtures before rollout.

implementation: Runtime evaluator for SOC alert/no-alert decisions.
Generated SIEM-ready evidence pack under data/evidence.
Human-readable docs page and MCP tool exposure.
selected_feature.reason: Enterprise buyers need agentic AI controls that land inside SOC operations, not only docs or standalone JSON evidence.

standards_alignment.coverage: Uses provider-neutral GenAI, model, tool, and retrieval attributes as the event fabric for SOC-ready agent detections.
standards_alignment.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for Generative AI Systems

standards_alignment.coverage: Anchors MCP JSON-RPC request ids, method names, tool calls, protocol version, session ids, and transport evidence used by detection rules.
standards_alignment.name: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for Model Context Protocol

standards_alignment.coverage: Maps confused deputy, token passthrough, SSRF, session hijacking, local server compromise, and scope minimization guidance into deployable SOC detections.
standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices

standards_alignment.coverage: Covers server-initiated user input requests, sensitive information restrictions, user approval, rate limiting, and clear requesting-server display.
standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Elicitation

standards_alignment.coverage: Prioritizes token exposure, scope creep, tool poisoning, command execution, insufficient authorization, lack of telemetry, shadow MCP servers, and context over-sharing.
standards_alignment.name: OWASP MCP Top 10

standards_alignment.coverage: Covers prompt injection, sensitive information disclosure, supply chain, improper output handling, excessive agency, vector weaknesses, and unbounded consumption.
standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025

standards_alignment.coverage: Maps SOC detections to Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage expectations for GAI monitoring, risk response, lifecycle evidence, and incident handling.
standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile

standards_alignment.coverage: Reinforces agent identity, least privilege, prompt/data separation, memory isolation, tool allowlists, organization controls, and recovery planning for MCP-backed agents.
standards_alignment.name: Google Cloud MCP AI Security and Safety

standards_alignment.coverage: Supports preserving visibility and control in AI deployment while democratizing defensive tools for trusted cyber operators.
standards_alignment.name: OpenAI Cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age

standards_alignment.coverage: Frames high-risk agentic attack chains, autonomous tool use, and threat-informed defensive chokepoints that SOC detections should observe.
standards_alignment.name: MITRE ATLAS OpenClaw Investigation

workflow_detection_overlays.title: Base Image and Container Layer Remediation
